---
title: "Publications"
---

## Peer-Reviewed Journal Articles

Kang, Bora. “Performative Exhaustion: Legibility, Complicity, and Limit in *A Gesture Life*.” *MELUS* 51.3 (forthcoming Jan. 2027).

Kang, Bora. “‘Whoever You Are to Yourself’: Conditional Hospitality and Affective Asymmetry in Ali Smith’s *Winter*.” *Journal of English Studies in Korea* 50 (2026): 5–31.

Kang, Bora. “From National Trauma to Transnational Testimony: Comfort Women Cinema and the Politics of Postmemory.” *The Journal of Modern British & American Language & Literature* 44.2 (2026): 25–46.

Kang, Bora. “The Chain of Refiguration: Reading Woolf-Cunningham-Daldry through Ricoeur’s Triple Mimesis.” *The Korean Association of Literature and Film* 27.1 (2026): 7–38.

## Book Project

*Affective Drift and Englishness from Woolf to Brexit: Literature, Film, and Media.*

Monograph proposal and sample chapters submitted to Edinburgh University Press.

## Manuscripts Under Review

“Heritage Aesthetics as Streaming’s Emotional Template.” *Television and New Media*. R&R invited; revised manuscript resubmitted.

“Feeling Brexit: Drift and Saturation in Ali Smith’s *Autumn* and *Winter*.” *Contemporary Women’s Writing*. R&R invited.

“Dignity as Discipline: Affective Loops and Post-Imperial Englishness in Ishiguro’s *The Remains of the Day*.” Under review.
